launchDefinition: Public beta is launched when a stranger on the public internet can: (1) reach the product on its own domain over HTTPS (not the founder's laptop), (2) create an account and sign in, (3) pay via live-mode Stripe checkout ($400/6mo or $600/12mo) and be entitlement-gated on it, (4) use /terminal with live market data in a fully isolated workspace — own alerts, drawings, scripts, layouts, watchlists, and MT5 WS events with verified zero cross-user leakage, (5) refresh and get the exact same workspace back via versioned autosave, (6) run a real screener (indicator/price/volume conditions across the symbol catalog, saved screens) and choose from ≥45 tested classic indicators, and (7) read public PulseScript documentation whose examples all run in the editor — while the founder's 48 live 1d EMA(5)×EMA(10) alerts keep firing and nightly tested backups + uptime monitoring run in production.

phases.name: Phase 1 — Multi-user foundation
phases.goal: Kill the single-user stub: real accounts, per-user data + WS scoping, workspace that survives refresh — without ever disturbing the founder's 48 live alerts. Folds in rebuild slices S2, S3, and S5 (they ARE launch blockers); defers S6/S7.
phases.exitCriteria: Two real accounts operate fully isolated (scripted cross-user REST requests all 403/404; two-browser WS test shows zero cross-user MT5/alert frames); workspace auto-restores identically after hard refresh; founder's 48 alerts verified firing under the migrated founder account; full test suite + typecheck green.

phases.name: Phase 2 — Revenue
phases.goal: Turn the fail-closed billing scaffold (apps/api/src/routes/billing.ts already 503s without STRIPE_SECRET_KEY) into working test-mode Stripe checkout, webhooks, and entitlement gating on the terminal.
phases.exitCriteria: Test-card purchase → webhook → /api/billing/status active with correct period end → terminal unlocked; unpaid account is blocked server-side and redirected to /pricing; cancel via Stripe billing portal reflects in status.

phases.name: Phase 3 — Hosted & reliable
phases.goal: Off the sleep-killed portable SSD onto monitored, backed-up production infrastructure that survives real networks. Folds in rebuild slice S4 (reconnect correctness matters once users aren't on localhost) and a load smoke that decides whether S6 (rAF tick batching, finding F1) must be pulled pre-launch.
phases.exitCriteria: Signup → live chart → create alert completed from an external network (phone LTE) on the domain over HTTPS/WSS; backup restore drill passed on staging; uptime alert fires when the API is killed; load smoke metrics within budget (or S6 explicitly scheduled before beta).

phases.name: Phase 4 — Launch surface (the user's three asks)
phases.goal: Ship exactly what was asked for beyond parity: more indicators (starting with the already-planned INC-10 batch), a real screener replacing the top-movers-only Scanner tab, and public user-facing PulseScript documentation.
phases.exitCriteria: Screener runs saved multi-condition indicator scans whose results are hand-verified against chart values; indicator registry ≥45 with fixture tests, all browsable/configurable; /docs/pulsescript live and linked from the editor, every code example runs clean.

phases.name: Phase 5 — Beta launch
phases.goal: Reduce sign-in friction (OAuth), prove the whole system on 10–20 real YouTube-audience users, then flip Stripe to live mode and announce publicly. Rebuild slices S6–S8 and punchlist INC-2/4/5/9/16/17/18 stay post-launch unless the load smoke said otherwise.
phases.exitCriteria: Every clause of the launch definition satisfied with pasted evidence; ≥10 external accounts used the product with zero isolation incidents in logs; top-3 beta-reported issues fixed and confirmed; first live-mode payment processed end-to-end; monitoring green for 48h after the announcement.

sessionBacklog.title: Rebuild S2 — async-state consistency (the repo's declared next slice)
sessionBacklog.deliverable: Shared PanelState primitives (loading/empty/error+retry) adopted by the Alerts Active list, Strategy ActiveList, Watchlists manager, Scanner, and Watchlist quotes; in-flight guards on PulseScript Save and MT5 token regen; error surfacing on PulseScript delete and the webhooks forward toggle (fixes audit P5–P9).
sessionBacklog.verify: Kill the API mid-session → every listed panel shows an inline error with a working Retry (screenshots); double-click PulseScript Save → exactly one POST in the network log; suite + typecheck green.

sessionBacklog.title: Real auth core — Auth.js credentials (roadmap #20, session file 12)
sessionBacklog.deliverable: Signup/login/logout with hashed passwords and a real session cookie; apps/api/src/auth.ts getUser() resolves the session instead of returning the hardcoded 'demo' row (audit S3); 401 on unauthenticated API access; login/signup pages.
sessionBacklog.verify: curl /api/alerts without a cookie → 401; create two accounts in the browser → each sees only its own (empty) alert/drawing/layout lists; suite + typecheck green.

sessionBacklog.title: Per-user isolation sweep + founder migration off 'demo'
sessionBacklog.deliverable: Two-account IDOR exercise across all resource routes (alerts, drawings, layouts, scripts, watchlists, signals, mt5, oanda, webhooks, share, custom-data, indicators, preferences, billing — the audit notes guards 'exist in shape but unexercised'); all demo-owned rows migrated to the founder's real account; demo-guard retired.
sessionBacklog.verify: Scripted cross-user GET/PUT/DELETE on every resource type → all 403/404 (output pasted); alert-engine log shows the 48 live alerts evaluating under the founder account post-migration; a real Telegram delivery observed unchanged.

sessionBacklog.title: Rebuild S5a — authenticated WebSocket + MT5 event scoping
sessionBacklog.deliverable: WS upgrade authenticates the session cookie (ws-gateway.ts:68/:73 no longer hardcodes userId:'demo' — audit S2); subscribe_mt5 filters mt5Store emitter events by the socket's userId (fixes the critical cross-user leak, audit S1, ws-gateway.ts:112-122); alert-event scoping re-verified with real distinct users.
sessionBacklog.verify: Two browsers on two accounts: MT5 activity on account A produces zero MT5 frames on B's socket (network-inspector evidence pasted); unauthenticated WS upgrade is rejected; existing WS tests green.

sessionBacklog.title: Rebuild S5b — API production hardening
sessionBacklog.deliverable: @fastify/helmet secure headers; /livez + /readyz split; correlation IDs in request logs; zod validation on PATCH /mt5/positions/:id (audit S5 — unvalidated SL/TP reaches the EA); calendar.ts safeParse → 400 not 500; signals.ts conditions schema replaces z.unknown() (audit S4–S6).
sessionBacklog.verify: curl output pasted for headers, /readyz, and a malformed PATCH → 400 with no EA-bound message in the bridge log; suite + typecheck green.

sessionBacklog.title: Rebuild S3 — versioned per-user workspace autosave/restore
sessionBacklog.deliverable: Debounced server-side autosave of a versioned workspace snapshot (panes/symbols/intervals/grid, indicator instances, overlay flags, pulse state, rail/dock chrome) per user, restored on load with migrations (fixes F3); named-layout Apply restores pane symbols/intervals too (P3); save/offline indicator in the top bar.
sessionBacklog.verify: Configure a 4-pane workspace with indicators + a running script → hard refresh → pixel-comparable screenshot pair shows identical state; second account is unaffected; a version-bump migration unit test passes.

sessionBacklog.title: Stripe checkout + webhooks, test mode (roadmap #21, session file 13)
sessionBacklog.deliverable: POST /api/billing/checkout creates real Stripe Checkout Sessions for pro_6m/pro_12m; signature-verified webhook endpoint handling checkout.session.completed and customer.subscription.updated/deleted writes the subscriptions table (replacing the current fail-closed 503 scaffold).
sessionBacklog.verify: Full test-mode purchase with a Stripe test card → /api/billing/status returns the plan active with the correct currentPeriodEnd (response pasted); webhook replay with a bad signature → 400.

sessionBacklog.title: Entitlement gating + billing/account UI
sessionBacklog.deliverable: Server-side entitlement check (active/trialing subscription — trial-vs-none decided by founder before this session) gating terminal APIs, with client redirect to /pricing; pricing-page buttons wired to checkout; account page showing plan/renewal/cancel via the Stripe billing portal.
sessionBacklog.verify: Unpaid account → API-blocked and redirected; paid test account → full terminal; cancel in the portal reflects in /api/billing/status and the account page (screenshots).

sessionBacklog.title: Production build + deploy runbook
sessionBacklog.deliverable: Env-driven web/API URLs and CORS origins (replacing the 127.0.0.1 pins), Next production build, Fastify prod mode, process supervision (docker-compose or PM2), SQLite WAL + data-directory convention (Node 26 for node:sqlite pinned), and a written deploy runbook.
sessionBacklog.verify: The full production stack boots locally from a clean clone with only env vars → /terminal streams live candles with 0 console errors (screenshot); runbook committed.

sessionBacklog.title: VPS go-live — domain, TLS, WS proxy, MT5 bridge exposure
sessionBacklog.deliverable: App running on a VPS in a region pre-validated for Binance WS/REST access; domain + TLS reverse proxy fronting web, API, and WSS; firewall; MT5 bridge :7878 exposed only with per-user token auth; all secrets (Telegram token, OANDA, Stripe) in server env.
sessionBacklog.verify: Signup → live chart → create-alert flow completed from an external network (phone LTE) on the domain; WSS frames observed; an MT5 EA connects to the hosted bridge with a fresh token (or the EA test is explicitly deferred in writing).

sessionBacklog.title: Backups + monitoring + restore drill
sessionBacklog.deliverable: Nightly offsite SQLite backup with a scripted restore; uptime monitoring on /readyz with alerting; disk/memory alerts; log retention using the correlation IDs from API-HARDEN.
sessionBacklog.verify: A real backup restored into a staging instance boots and serves the founder's data; killing the API triggers the uptime alert (notification screenshot).

sessionBacklog.title: Rebuild S4 — client network hardening
sessionBacklog.deliverable: Typed ApiError in lib/api.ts, AbortController adoption on panel fetches, WS sequence/gap guard with refetch-on-gap, and overlay-set-preserving resubscribe so footprint/tape/DOM survive reconnect (fixes F4/F5).
sessionBacklog.verify: Forced WS disconnect with footprint on → overlay resumes without reload (screenshot); injected sequence gap → refetch observed in the network log; new unit tests green.

sessionBacklog.title: Concurrent-user load smoke + perf gate
sessionBacklog.deliverable: A script driving ~25 concurrent authenticated WS clients plus REST traffic across symbols against the deployed stack; recorded p95 API latency, WS frame delivery, server memory; a written go/no-go on whether F1 (per-tick full ChartPane re-render — rebuild S6) must be pulled pre-launch.
sessionBacklog.verify: Metrics pasted from the actual run vs a stated budget (e.g. p95 < 300ms, no OOM, no dropped subscriptions); if over budget, an S6 session is inserted into the backlog before BETA-CLOSED.

sessionBacklog.title: Indicator coverage batch 1 — DEMA/TEMA/VWMA (punchlist INC-10, session file 06)
sessionBacklog.deliverable: Three new registry entries in @supercharts/indicators with fixture unit tests; browser/legend/Data-Window wiring via the existing registry-driven pipeline.
sessionBacklog.verify: Vitest values match hand-computed fixtures (output pasted); each indicator added, configured, and screenshotted on live BTCUSDT.

sessionBacklog.title: Indicator coverage batch 2 — remaining classics from the TV-parity audit gap list
sessionBacklog.deliverable: The next set of missing classic indicators from the parity audit (.audit/tv-parity/PUNCHLIST.md gap list) added to the registry with tests, bringing the registry to ≥45 entries; no rebuild of existing ones.
sessionBacklog.verify: Per-indicator fixture tests green; registry count pasted from a command; each new indicator added and configured in the browser.

sessionBacklog.title: Screener query engine (server)
sessionBacklog.deliverable: POST /api/scanner/query: declarative filter conditions (price, change%, volume, and indicator values computed via @supercharts/indicators over cached candles) evaluated across the 48-symbol catalog on a chosen timeframe, with result caching; the existing /scanner/top-movers endpoint kept.
sessionBacklog.verify: Unit tests over synthetic candles; a live curl query (e.g. RSI(14) < 30 on 1h) whose results are hand-verified against the chart's Data Window values for 2 symbols (both pasted).

sessionBacklog.title: Screener UI + saved screens (replaces the top-movers-only Scanner tab)
sessionBacklog.deliverable: Scanner tab upgrade in right-rail.tsx: condition-builder rows (metric/operator/value/timeframe), sortable result columns, click-to-load symbol on the active pane, per-user server-persisted saved screens, honest venue-limitation states for non-Binance data.
sessionBacklog.verify: Build and run a screen in the browser → results match the SCAN-1 curl for the same query; a saved screen survives refresh and re-login (screenshots).

sessionBacklog.title: Public PulseScript documentation
sessionBacklog.deliverable: User-facing docs under the web app (/docs/pulsescript): getting started, full language reference sourced from docs/pulsescript-language.md (pulse 1 header, no-let assignment, colon bodies, persist/when/draw/mark), ta./math. stdlib reference, inputs/drawing/alerts/onTf pages, runnable examples; linked from the editor and the landing page.
sessionBacklog.verify: Browser navigation through every page; every code example pasted into the live editor runs clean (run-summary console output pasted for each).

sessionBacklog.title: Google OAuth + account polish (completes roadmap #20)
sessionBacklog.deliverable: Google sign-in via Auth.js alongside credentials; account page (email, display name, linked provider); a decided password-reset story (minimal SMTP reset or a documented manual process for beta).
sessionBacklog.verify: Fresh Google sign-up produces an isolated workspace; an existing credentials account is untouched; both login paths screenshotted on the deployed domain.

sessionBacklog.title: Closed beta with 10–20 YouTube-audience users
sessionBacklog.deliverable: Invited cohort on live infrastructure (comped or test-coupon subscriptions), a feedback channel, and the top reported issues fixed as micro-sessions under the one-increment protocol.
sessionBacklog.verify: ≥10 real external accounts in the users table (count pasted); zero cross-user isolation errors in production logs over the beta window; top-3 reported issues fixed and confirmed by their reporters.

sessionBacklog.title: Public beta launch
sessionBacklog.deliverable: Stripe switched to live mode (live keys + live webhook endpoint), launch announcement video published on the channel, landing CTA live, support email + public status/uptime page linked from the footer.
sessionBacklog.verify: One real live-mode purchase completes end-to-end (checkout → webhook → entitled terminal access); monitoring green for 48h post-announcement; the launch video is live.

risks: Live-alert regression during the demo→founder auth migration: the 48 production 1d EMA(5)×EMA(10) alerts are the founder's own trading signals — AUTH-2 must migrate and re-verify Telegram delivery, never drop or resubscribe-dirty them.

MT5 TCP bridge on the public internet: :7878 is a raw TCP protocol with only per-user token auth planned and no EA-side TLS story — if hardening slips, restrict hosted MT5 automation to the founder/invited users for beta rather than delaying launch.

Binance geo-blocking: Binance's public API blocks several regions (including US IPs) — the VPS region must be validated for both WS and REST before committing (explicit DEPLOY-2 gate), or the order-flow feature set silently dies in production.

node:sqlite single-writer + Node 26 host requirement: fine at beta scale with WAL, but there is no defined migration path to Postgres/litestream if writes contend — nightly tested backups (DEPLOY-3) are the safety net, and the scaling decision is deliberately deferred.

F1 per-tick full ChartPane re-render (2289 lines × N panes, no rAF batching) is unmeasured under real load — LOAD-SMOKE is the explicit gate; if it fails, rebuild slice S6 must be inserted before BETA-CLOSED, adding ~1-2 sessions.
React 19 RC + Next 15 in a paid production product: pin exact versions at DEPLOY-1 and treat any RC upgrade as its own verified session, not a drive-by.
Pricing friction: $400/6mo with no trial may kill beta conversion — the trial/coupon decision is a founder call required before STRIPE-2, not a build task.

Credentials-only auth until AUTH-OAUTH and no email infrastructure means no self-serve password reset early on — acceptable for closed beta, must be resolved (OAuth + reset story) before public launch.

Solo-founder capacity: beta support and incident response compete with the one-verified-increment-per-session protocol — cap the closed beta at ~20 users and keep the fabrication-tripwire/session-hygiene rules in force.

Scope creep: rebuild slices S6–S8, punchlist INC-2/4/5/9/16/17/18, iframe embeds (#18), multi-user shared workspaces (#19), and mobile/PWA/WASM (#23–25) are consciously post-launch — none of them block the launch definition and none should absorb a pre-launch session.
